::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
## Phase B vs. Phase C: how the hedge is parameterised

Phase B specifies the principal book's pre-purchase as a single ratio
$\alpha \in [0, 1]$ and assumes the tokens were bought today at $S_0$.
Phase C replaces that with the **raw inventory state** $(k,\,
C_{\mathrm{basis}})$: the kVCM tokens on hand plus what they cost.

The Phase B setting is the special case
$$
k = \alpha \cdot N \cdot P, \qquad
C_{\mathrm{basis}} = \alpha \cdot N \cdot P \cdot S_0.
$$
Phase C's form lifts the "bought today at $S_0$" assumption so
inventory acquired earlier at a different price can be studied
directly. The back-to-back book $\Pi_{\mathrm{b2b}}$ is unchanged
across phases and is the shared anchor for cross-reading the summary
tables.

| Phase B | Phase C equivalent |
| --- | --- |
| Hedge ratio $\alpha$ | Coverage $\tau = \min(1,\, k/(N \cdot P))$ |
| $\Pi_\alpha$ | $\Pi_{\mathrm{cust}}$ (coincides when $C_{\mathrm{basis}} = k \cdot S_0$) |
:::
